body.title: High-inertia gates for shared-state tables (machines, rules) + redirection tactic
body.result: Shipped in 443d00da. Table-level inertia policy in lab/utility_closet/table_inertia.py — asymmetric: empty/sparse (<3 rows) → LOW + redirection msg pointing at fill path; populated (>=10) → HIGH + requires_approval; mid → MEDIUM. Reason: encourages initial fill (redirection), protects against regression wipes (HIGH once populated). Wired infra.machines via machine_manager.machines_inertia() + machines_row_count(). Rules table left for follow-up — no rules_manager module yet (approval notes acknowledged). 12 tests in test_table_inertia.py.
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Shared-state tables (machines, rules) have gone blank multiple times — either from schema changes, data loss, or incomplete initialization. This ticket assigns HIGH-inertia gates to these tables and implements a redirection tactic: when Igor tries to edit a load-bearing table (machines, rules, or similar) and the table is unpopulated or corrupt, instead of allowing the edit to proceed unguarded, surface a positive redirection (e.g., 'I need to populate machines first — let me check what we have and fill gaps') rather than failing silently.

Related to, but separate from, memory-inertia work (which exists: memories have HIGH-inertia already). This ticket focuses on non-memory schema tables and the redirection pattern.

Not required before pass-2 remaining candidates, but same level of work (hardening shared state for autonomous edits).

**Affected files:** lab/utility_closet/machine_manager.py (machines tables — schema, inertia scoring), wild_igor/igor/cognition/rules_manager.py or similar (rules table — needs discovery if not yet exists), wild_igor/igor/tools/self_edit.py (HIGH-inertia gate → check if table is populated before allowing edit, emit redirection if not).

**Design rules:** HIGH-inertia check applies; docs-in-code applies (any new redirection logic gets docstring explaining the gate + positive frame); test-plan-or-why-not.

**Scope boundary:** IN — assign HIGH-inertia to machines+rules tables, wire redirection tactic into self_edit.py gate logic so unpopulated tables trigger redirect instead of silent failure. OUT — memory-inertia (separate, exists), new schema design, fixing past data loss root causes (separate diagnosis).

**Test plan:** (1) Unit test: machines_manager / rules_manager with empty/corrupt table → inertia check returns HIGH + reason; (2) Integration test: self_edit.py receives HIGH-inertia request on empty machines table → responds with redirection narrative (suggested next steps), not an error; (3) Happy path: populated table + HIGH-inertia → normal approval flow (no regression).
body.approved_plan: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/kernel.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/kernel.py
body.approval_notes: Approved 2026-04-23 with amended framing: HIGH-inertia gate applies ONCE POPULATED (protects against regression wipes); low-inertia-with-redirection while empty/sparse (encourages initial fill). Same pattern both tables.
